Package component.mover

Class Mover

java.lang.Object
component.mover.Mover
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tippable, GridIndexable
Direct Known Subclasses:
Conveyor, Delay, FlipFlop, ParityFilter, RedBlackFilter
public abstract class Mover extends Object implements GridIndexable, Tippable
The Mover class is the abstract foundation for all tiles that control card movement across the grid.

It provides the mathematical translation from abstract Direction to grid coordinates and manages the rotation state of the tile.

  • Constructor Summary

    Description

    Constructs a Mover at a default position with a specified rotation.

  • Constructor Details

    • Mover

      public Mover(Direction rot)
      Constructs a Mover at a default position with a specified rotation.
      Parameters:
      rot - The initial Direction the mover faces.

  • Method Details

    • getTranslationFromDirection

      public static GridPos getTranslationFromDirection(Direction direction)
      Converts a Direction enum into a coordinate translation vector.
      Parameters:
      direction - The direction to translate.
      Returns:
      A GridPos representing the delta (e.g., UP returns 0, -1).

    • getDirectionStateless

      public abstract Direction getDirectionStateless()
      Retrieves the current direction without triggering any internal state changes. Useful for UI previews and pathfinding simulations.
      Returns:
      The Direction the mover currently points toward.

    • getDirection

      public Direction getDirection()
      Retrieves the direction for actual movement execution.

      Subclasses with state (like FlipFlops) should override this to trigger their internal logic when a card is moved.

      Returns:
      The Direction the card should move this tick.

    • getValidOutputDirections

      public abstract Direction[] getValidOutputDirections()
      Returns an array of all possible directions this mover could potentially send a card, used for rendering exit markers.
      Returns:
      An array of Direction constants.

    • rotate

      public void rotate()
      Increments the rotation of the mover to the next clockwise direction.

    • getRotation

      public Direction getRotation()
      Returns:
      The primary Direction this mover is oriented toward.

    • setRotation

      public void setRotation(Direction rot)
      Sets the mover's rotation. If Direction.STAY is passed, it defaults to Direction.UP to ensure cards always have a vector.
      Parameters:
      rot - The new Direction.

    • getGridPos

      public GridPos getGridPos()
      Specified by:
      getGridPos in interface GridIndexable
      Returns:
      The current GridPos of the object.

    • setGridPos

      public void setGridPos(GridPos point)
      Specified by:
      setGridPos in interface GridIndexable
      Parameters:
      point - The new GridPos to assign to this object.

    • isBlocking

      public boolean isBlocking()
      Specified by:
      isBlocking in interface GridIndexable
      Returns:
      Always false for movers, as they are designed to be passed through.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Resets the mover's internal state for level restarts.

    • getTooltip

      public Tooltip getTooltip()
      Specified by:
      getTooltip in interface Tippable
      Returns:
      The Tooltip associated with this object.

    • getMoverTooltip

      public static Tooltip getMoverTooltip()
      Provides a generic tooltip for the Mover category.
      Returns:
      A Tooltip with the Mover color theme (OliveDrab).
